title: Discovery & Validation
objective: Turn the analysis workstreams (Keycloak claim mapping, MinIO/SQL provisioning, Webex licensing, Tarseem `doctor`, Arabic-FTS quality) into signed-off answers before coding; complete the planning package.
exit_criteria: All PH-1-blocking `OQ-` resolved; branch strategy agreed; environments accessible.

title: MVP Governance
objective: Deliver the complete architecture-governance loop: topic intake → backlog → agenda → meeting → voting → decision → action, with Keycloak identity (RBAC + per-topic ABAC), in-app notifications, full EN/AR + RTL, light/dark, append-only hash-chained audit, basic dashboards, FTS, and a typed traceability panel; plus risk creation and basic dependency recording.
exit_criteria: All Must FRs implemented, tested, signed off; zero Sev-1; one full meeting cycle on the platform; staging stable ≥5 business days.

title: Governance Expansion
objective: Full governance maturity: ADRs + Architecture Invariants, Research/Keystone import, Tarseem diagram sidecar + dependency-graph visualization, Templates + Knowledge wiki, the Webex adapter (notifications + recording/transcript), advanced traceability (impact analysis), expanded reporting, notification preferences + digests, Guest/Presenter access, CoI tracking, Gantt view, audit export + retention config.
exit_criteria: Should FRs implemented + signed off; Tarseem sidecar stable 48h; zero Sev-1. Entry gate: PH-1 exit met.

title: Research & Knowledge
objective: AI-assisted transcript extraction (candidate-only, human-reviewed — OWASP LLM01), transcript FTS, email channel (when SMTP available), KPI/health dashboard, traceability-matrix export, invariant exception workflow, bulk topic ops, advanced analytics.
exit_criteria: Committed Could FRs implemented + tested; AI off by default; privacy review signed off. Entry gate: PH-2 exit met.

title: Cross-cutting hardening & release
objective: Harden and release what PH-1 and PH-2 built: an OWASP ASVS L2 security sweep, testing hardening, production deployment with backup and warm-standby restore, and a final acceptance audit against the release gates. RECONCILIATION: the roadmap classifies P16–P19 as cross-cutting under no phase; PH-4 exists solely to satisfy the NOT NULL foreign key on slices.phase_id; the two records are consistent, not conflicting. See DEC-029 and SC-001.
exit_criteria: All four cross-cutting slices shipped and merged to main with CI green; the final release-readiness audit produced and its verdict recorded. Met: P16, P17, P18 and P19 all shipped; the audit returned a conditional no-go pending operator go-live actions, not further build work.
